jokes.text: The only thing I am crazy about is a magnificent, pregnant manta ray named Ann Perkins.
jokes.setup: Someone calls Chris crazy
jokes.punchline: Chris responds with an elaborate, over-the-top metaphor comparing Ann to a pregnant manta ray
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris Traeger character moment - his overly enthusiastic, poetic language applied to something mundane. The manta ray comparison is both unexpected and weirdly beautiful.

jokes.text: I am ranking topics of conversation for our next phone call... Infinity scarves or whether or not it's worth it for me to get Showtime?
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions ranking conversation topics
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity and mundane nature of the topics she's prioritizing
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie overthinking - turning casual conversation into a ranked priority system with hilariously trivial topics.

jokes.text: I mean, I can talk about Sandra Bullock skirt length.
jokes.setup: Ben offers to be a sounding board for female topics
jokes.punchline: His awkward, masculine attempt to relate results in a weirdly specific and inappropriate topic
jokes.explanation: Ben's sincere but completely tone-deaf attempt to connect with female conversation topics. The specificity of 'Sandra Bullock skirt length' is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: No, no, babe, you can't--you would just embarrass yourself. Plus, every time I start talking to you, five seconds later, I want to jump your bones.
jokes.setup: Ben offers to help with girl talk
jokes.punchline: Leslie's blunt rejection followed by her oversharing about their sex life
jokes.explanation: Leslie's characteristic oversharing and directness. The transition from dismissal to TMI is appropriately awkward.

jokes.text: I love being a father, but there are a few things I miss. Silence, the absence of noise, one single moment undisturbed by the sounds of a children's TV program called Doc McStuffins.
jokes.setup: Ron talks about missing things as a father
jokes.punchline: His poetic description of missing silence, undercut by the specific annoyance of Doc McStuffins
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson - his philosophical, almost poetic language applied to the mundane frustrations of parenthood. The specificity of Doc McStuffins grounds it in reality.

jokes.text: There is no quiet anymore. There is only Doc McStuffins.
jokes.setup: Ron's continued lament about noise
jokes.punchline: The dramatic finality of 'There is only Doc McStuffins' delivered in Ron's deadpan style
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly economical and quotable. Ron's existential despair expressed through a children's TV show creates perfect comedic contrast.

jokes.text: Shotgun! I call shotgun. Where are we going? Doesn't matter. Shotgun. Shotgun on all rides for the rest of the day. For the rest of my life. In any car! Ha ha! I just faced you suckers!
jokes.setup: Andy volunteers to go with Ron
jokes.punchline: His childish excitement escalating to claiming shotgun for life in any car
jokes.explanation: Pure Andy Dwyer - his childlike enthusiasm and inability to understand boundaries. The escalation from one car ride to 'all cars for life' is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Is the tent guy left-handed or right-handed? I just need to know which way to part my hair to get us a better price. It's a business theory I'm working on.
jokes.setup: Tom asks about the tent guy's handedness
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous connection between hair parting direction and business negotiations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom Haverford - his pseudo-sophisticated business theories that are completely absurd. The specificity of the hair parting makes it memorable.

jokes.text: A straightforward deal? Why didn't you tell me? I don't have my straightforward deal fedora on me. We gotta stop by my storage unit on the way up.
jokes.setup: Ben mentions it's a straightforward deal
jokes.punchline: Tom's implication that he has different hats for different types of business deals
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - Tom's ridiculous over-preparation and the idea of having specialized fedoras for different business scenarios is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Remember you said that when Larry's farting up the car.
jokes.setup: Ben defends inviting Larry
jokes.punchline: Tom's crude but probably accurate prediction about Larry
jokes.explanation: Standard Larry mockery - functional but not particularly creative. Gets the job done with basic physical humor.

jokes.text: Larry isn't that bad. He's nice. And frankly, I don't care who knows that I said that, as long as no one knows that I said that.
jokes.setup: Ben defends Larry
jokes.punchline: The immediate contradiction of saying he doesn't care who knows while immediately caring who knows
jokes.explanation: Nice logical contradiction delivered with perfect timing. Shows Ben's awareness of the social dynamics while still wanting to be kind.

jokes.text: Listen to me very carefully. I have not been taken. I know that's always your first fear when I'm not available, but this is not a Liam Neeson Taken scenario.
jokes.setup: Ann leaving a voicemail explaining her absence
jokes.punchline: Her knowledge that Leslie immediately assumes kidnapping scenarios
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing how well Ann knows Leslie's paranoid tendencies. The Taken reference is specific and relatable.

jokes.text: That's exactly what they'd make you say. Also, no one's making me say this. I am a free woman, untaken, simply going about my business.
jokes.setup: Ann's denial of being taken
jokes.punchline: Her continued denials that sound increasingly like what a kidnapped person would say
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the kidnapping joke. Ann's awareness of how suspicious her denials sound creates nice meta-humor.

jokes.text: What's good, baby? What's going down in Donna town? What's the haps in Meagle-wood?
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to bond with Donna
jokes.punchline: Her awkward, try-hard slang that sounds forced and unnatural
jokes.explanation: Leslie trying too hard to sound cool with forced slang. The 'Meagle-wood' portmanteau is particularly cringeworthy in a good way.

jokes.text: I'm listening to Jaleel Or No Deal. It's a podcast where the kid who played Urkel reviews old Deal Or No Deal episodes. It's pointless, and I love it.
jokes.setup: Donna explaining what she's listening to
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and pointless nature of this fictional podcast
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - the specificity of combining Jaleel White with Deal or No Deal is perfectly random yet somehow believable. Donna's honest assessment makes it funnier.

jokes.text: What's the 411, little mama? What's the hot goss? Who you crushing on these days?
jokes.setup: Leslie continuing her forced slang with April
jokes.punchline: The cringey outdated slang getting progressively worse
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Leslie's awkward slang attempts. The escalation to 'little mama' and 'hot goss' makes it appropriately uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Ew, my husband, weirdo. Can I get back to work now? Thanks. Bye.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks who April is crushing on
jokes.punchline: April's disgusted reaction to the question and dismissive shutdown
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response - simultaneously answering the question while expressing complete disdain for the interaction. The quick dismissal is efficiently brutal.

jokes.text: You sound like a tampon commercial.
jokes.setup: Leslie talks about 'lady time'
jokes.punchline: April's blunt, accurate observation about Leslie's language
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered April line - cutting through Leslie's enthusiasm with brutal honesty. The comparison is unexpected but spot-on.

jokes.text: Wait, I thought you weren't gonna do Galentine's day this year because that she-beast moved away. I believe her name was Satan?
jokes.setup: April commenting on Leslie's Galentine's Day plans
jokes.punchline: Her casual reference to Ann as 'she-beast' and 'Satan'
jokes.explanation: Classic April - her dislike of Ann expressed through casual cruelty delivered in deadpan style. The escalation from 'she-beast' to 'Satan' works well.

jokes.text: Side note, do not Google that phrase.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions 'women's-only marathon bonding session with tons of whipped cream'
jokes.punchline: Her awareness of how that phrase could be interpreted online
jokes.explanation: Meta joke acknowledging the unintended sexual implications of her innocent description. Leslie's awareness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Do you know what my social security number is? Or if I'm allergic to anything? I don't like corn. Should I put that down? Or will that be confusing? Because I do like candy corn.
jokes.setup: Andy needing medical information
jokes.punchline: His confusion about corn vs candy corn as an allergy
jokes.explanation: Pure Andy logic - treating a food preference as a medical allergy and being confused by the corn/candy corn distinction. Perfectly childlike reasoning.

jokes.text: Ron, look. I found all three differences. Those are two completely different pictures.
jokes.setup: Andy claiming to find differences in pictures
jokes.punchline: Ron's deadpan observation that they're completely different pictures
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction - Andy's misunderstanding of the game met with Ron's matter-of-fact correction. The absurdity is grounded in character.

jokes.text: Oh, whoa. Five-second rule. No, ahh. Come on, we better get you to a dentist.
jokes.setup: Andy hurts his tooth
jokes.punchline: His attempt to apply the five-second rule to a dental injury
jokes.explanation: Andy's illogical application of food rules to dental trauma. Shows his childlike thinking process under stress.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, Larry. Your tuna fish sandwich stunk up my suit. Gayle made it for me. Irrelevant.
jokes.setup: Tom complaining about Larry's sandwich smell
jokes.punchline: Tom's dismissal of Larry's innocent explanation
jokes.explanation: Standard Larry harassment - Tom's superficial concerns vs Larry's simple explanations. The 'irrelevant' dismissal is characteristically cruel.

jokes.text: Who's this little genie right here?
jokes.setup: Harvey meeting Tom at the tent store
jokes.punchline: The tent guy's assessment of Tom as a 'little genie'
jokes.explanation: Good visual/character humor about Tom's appearance and demeanor

jokes.text: I got over 1,300 tents in this warehouse alone. 900 of those are over 2,000 square feet, 80 of 'em are striped, and exactly 4 of 'em were once rented by Miss Rene Russo.
jokes.setup: Harvey describing his tent expertise
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific statistics ending with the random Rene Russo detail
jokes.explanation: Great character establishment - Harvey's obsessive knowledge combined with the random celebrity detail shows he's both professional and eccentric.

jokes.text: I was gonna go with my boyfriend, but he couldn't come because he decided to stay with his wife.
jokes.setup: Shauna talking about her solo trip to Rome
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation that her boyfriend is married
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually - Shauna's matter-of-fact revelation of being the other woman. The casual tone makes it more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Rome's played out. Have y'all been to Kuala Lumpur? I go all the time. Where do you think I got that crystal Buddha head above my jacuzzi?
jokes.setup: Shauna mentioning Rome
jokes.punchline: Donna's casual one-upmanship with exotic travel and luxury details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna - effortlessly outdoing someone while revealing her luxurious lifestyle. The Buddha head detail adds character specificity.

jokes.text: And then we got Evelyn, aka fake Ann. I don't know her last name. Honestly we just needed bodies.
jokes.setup: Leslie introducing the Galentine's Day candidates
jokes.punchline: Her brutally honest assessment of Evelyn as just a warm body
jokes.explanation: Leslie's characteristic over-honesty combined with her desperation to fill the Ann void. The 'fake Ann' nickname is particularly harsh.

jokes.text: But please answer them with complete honesty 'cause I'll be able to tell if you're lying.
jokes.setup: Leslie introducing the Q&A as 'just for fun'
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing it's actually a serious test with lie detection
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's intensity disguised as casual fun

jokes.text: Well, for live tweeting, it's Scandal. For binge watching, it's Scandal. But for fashion, it's actually Scandal. My answer is Scandal.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks about favorite TV show
jokes.punchline: Donna's elaborate breakdown that all leads to the same answer
jokes.explanation: Good structure - building expectation of variety before revealing it's all the same show. Shows Donna's sophistication and obsession.

jokes.text: Ooh, I love watching Russian traffic accidents on YouTube while I play children's music at the wrong RPM.
jokes.setup: April asked about her favorite TV show
jokes.punchline: Her disturbing combination of violent content with corrupted children's music
jokes.explanation: Perfectly April - dark, specific, and weirdly artistic. The combination of Russian dash cam footage with slowed children's music is brilliantly disturbing and creative.

jokes.text: I like beauty pageants. Everyone is so happy and perfect.
jokes.setup: Shauna sharing her TV preferences
jokes.punchline: The ironic contrast between her messy life and attraction to perfection
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Shauna's attraction to artificial perfection contrasts with her complicated personal life. Shows her escapist tendencies.

jokes.text: I like Diagnosis Murder. And skin flicks.
jokes.setup: Ethel sharing her TV preferences
jokes.punchline: The unexpected combination of a wholesome medical show with adult films
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion - the sweet elderly lady casually mentioning adult content alongside Dick Van Dyke. The economy and shock value work perfectly.

jokes.text: The correct answer for favorite TV show is Friday Night Lights.
jokes.setup: After hearing everyone's TV preferences
jokes.punchline: Leslie's declaration that there's only one correct answer
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie - treating subjective preferences as objective facts. Shows her controlling nature in friendship evaluation.

jokes.text: The correct answer for favorite TV show is Friday Night Lights.
jokes.setup: Everyone giving different TV show answers
jokes.punchline: Leslie revealing there was actually only one correct answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment showing her controlling nature and predetermined answers

jokes.text: Well, you need flaps, bro. A tent without flaps is basically a parachute.
jokes.setup: Ben questioning the flap tax
jokes.punchline: Harvey's oddly compelling but ridiculous tent philosophy
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic - Harvey's passionate tent expertise creates a weirdly convincing but ridiculous argument. The parachute comparison is memorably bizarre.

jokes.text: Shh. Keep your voice down, please, there's tents here.
jokes.setup: Ben raising his voice about tent costs
jokes.punchline: Harvey treating tents like they can be damaged by loud voices
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Harvey's reverence for tents extends to protecting them from noise. The absurdity is delivered with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: Now, look, you can argue with me all you want, but for every line item that you two idiots disagree with, there's gonna be a $50 discussion surcharge.
jokes.setup: Ben and Tom questioning the contract
jokes.punchline: Harvey charging them for the act of questioning charges
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Harvey's absurd business practices reach peak ridiculousness. The meta-charge for questioning charges is brilliantly circular.

jokes.text: What's the price now? $80 more. I liked it better the other way. Does have an effect, though.
jokes.setup: Tom tries his hair-parting business theory
jokes.punchline: It actually has an effect but makes things worse
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Tom's earlier absurd theory, with the twist that it works but backfires. Shows the writers thought through the setup.

jokes.text: Do you know what my social security number is? Or if I'm allergic to anything? I don't like corn. Should I put that down? Or will that be confusing? Because I do like candy corn.
jokes.setup: Andy at the dentist office
jokes.punchline: His continued confusion about basic medical forms and corn logic
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier corn confusion, but feels somewhat repetitive. Still works as character consistency but less fresh.

jokes.text: All I wanted was two hours where I got to forget that I was a parent.
jokes.setup: Ron dealing with Andy's childish behavior
jokes.punchline: His realization that he's basically babysitting another child
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Ron's desire for escape from parenting responsibilities met with Andy's childlike neediness. The irony is beautifully constructed.

jokes.text: I've gotten two annulments. One for pleasure, and one to cap off a long con I was running against Keith Sweat.
jokes.setup: Donna sharing something personal during Q&A
jokes.punchline: The casually mentioned criminal activity against a R&B singer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Donna moment - the casual revelation of conning Keith Sweat is so specific and unexpected. The 'for pleasure' vs criminal motivation split is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I'm writing a memoir based on my blog. It's called Tweeping Up Appearances, and it's about my journey to find happiness by smiling through the pain.
jokes.setup: Shauna sharing her writing project
jokes.punchline: The terrible pun title that perfectly captures her shallow optimism
jokes.explanation: Good character work - the terrible pun title reflects Shauna's superficial approach to serious topics. Shows her lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: It's simply a way for me to figure out which one of you is the best.
jokes.setup: Leslie caught with friendship rankings
jokes.punchline: Her honest admission of the ranking's purpose
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie - her inability to lie or sugarcoat creates maximum social damage. The directness makes it funnier and more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: According to her, it's the best view.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining Gayle watching him clean windshields
jokes.punchline: The unintentional sexual implication of Gayle's perspective
jokes.explanation: Innocent Larry inadvertently creating sexual innuendo through his description of Gayle's behavior. Works through Larry's obliviousness.

jokes.text: Gayle always has me do the windshield. She likes to stand behind me and watch. According to her, it's the best view.
jokes.setup: Larry washing windshield and being nice
jokes.punchline: The sweet but awkward detail about Gayle watching him
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Larry and Gayle's relationship dynamic

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I'm just not used to people from the office asking me things. It's kind of scary. But in a good way, though.
jokes.setup: Ben showing genuine interest in Larry's family
jokes.punchline: Larry's pathetic gratitude for basic human interaction
jokes.explanation: Genuinely sad moment that's also funny - Larry's surprise at being treated like a human being. Shows how badly he's usually treated.

jokes.text: Yeah. Yeah, it was loud. Smells like a hippopotamus took a dump on a skunk.
jokes.setup: Larry covering for Ben's kindness by claiming he farted
jokes.punchline: His surprisingly vivid and elaborate description of the fictional fart
jokes.explanation: Larry protecting Ben's reputation by embellishing the fart story. The animal comparison is surprisingly creative and specific.

jokes.text: Well, my grandmother was missing that tooth, and she was the most beautiful woman on her oil rig.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why his missing tooth looks fine
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation about his grandmother working on an oil rig
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - the unexpected detail about grandma's work environment delivered as if it's completely normal. Great character backstory reveal.

jokes.text: That's why you know even though you're not my dad, you're like an old brother to me. Like a cool uncle, but old. Like a grandpa. But cool like a son.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to define his relationship with Ron
jokes.punchline: His logic completely breaking down and becoming circular
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy - his attempt to categorize relationships becomes increasingly confused and contradictory. Shows his childlike reasoning process.

jokes.text: That's right, dickweed. You wanna talk deal? Because my prices have gone up ever so slightly since I saw you last.
jokes.setup: Harvey revealing he owns the coffee shop too
jokes.punchline: His petty escalation and casual profanity
jokes.explanation: Harvey's pettiness and business monopoly escalating. The casual profanity shows his true colors when he has leverage.

jokes.text: Yes. I own every tent store in Southern Indiana. Tent Town, Rent Ten Tents, the Tent Offensive, and Ace Tentura Tent Detective.
jokes.setup: Harvey revealing his business empire
jokes.punchline: The increasingly ridiculous tent-themed business names
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay escalation - each tent pun gets more elaborate and ridiculous. 'Ace Tentura' is particularly clever, combining Ace Ventura with tent.

jokes.text: I also own a chick-fil-a franchise, but that's not doing so hot right now. Whatever.
jokes.setup: Harvey listing his business empire
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of his struggling fast food business
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Harvey's casual attitude toward business failures

jokes.text: I also own a chick-fil-a franchise, but that's not doing so hot right now. Whatever.
jokes.setup: Harvey listing his business empire
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of his failing Chick-fil-A
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - shows Harvey's business ventures aren't all successful. The casual dismissal of failure adds to his character.

jokes.text: You've reached the Tentagon. How can I help you, buttface?
jokes.setup: Tom calling another tent store
jokes.punchline: Another Harvey business with Pentagon/tent wordplay and his rude greeting
jokes.explanation: Great callback with another clever tent pun. 'Tentagon' is inspired wordplay, and the immediate hostility is funny escalation.

jokes.text: Although, when I was in college I found out that my dad had a second family. Well, we were the second family, technically.
jokes.setup: Shauna bonding over being an only child
jokes.punchline: The dark revelation about family secrets delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Good character depth - Shauna's casual revelation of family trauma shows why she makes poor choices. The 'technically' correction makes it darker.

jokes.text: Although there is a tiny part of me that always thought it was just Chris talking into a mirror.
jokes.setup: Shauna suggesting Leslie see Chris's therapist
jokes.punchline: Her theory about Chris's therapy sessions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight - perfectly captures Chris's self-absorbed nature. The image of him having therapy sessions with his own reflection is spot-on.

jokes.text: Why would you do that? Oh, right. 'Cause calories.
jokes.setup: Andy eating peanut brittle with a hurt tooth
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical calorie logic for making his injury worse
jokes.explanation: Pure Andy logic - applying food rules illogically to justify bad decisions. Ron's incredulous response sells the absurdity.

jokes.text: It's called Sweaty Roger's Pants Tent.
jokes.setup: Looking for alternative tent stores
jokes.punchline: The gross-sounding store name
jokes.explanation: Mild gross-out humor with pun elements - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Nobody can match your ethnic hybrid energy.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining why other friends don't measure up
jokes.punchline: The awkward phrasing of Ann's mixed heritage
jokes.explanation: Leslie's awkward but well-meaning attempt to celebrate diversity

jokes.text: You shut your mouth. You have all the strengths.
jokes.setup: Ann suggesting others have unique strengths
jokes.punchline: Leslie's aggressive denial of any weakness in Ann
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie - her inability to accept any criticism of Ann, even positive. The aggressive tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Yeah, 'cause Riggins is a criminal. He took the fall for his brother. Okay, he didn't watch out for his brother. You know, I'm not having this conversation with you again. That family is garbage.
jokes.setup: Ann and Leslie disagreeing about Friday Night Lights characters
jokes.punchline: Leslie's passionate hatred of the Riggins family escalating to calling them garbage
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Leslie's moral absolutism extended to fictional characters. The escalating criticism is appropriately passionate.

jokes.text: Yeah, 'cause Riggins is a criminal.
jokes.setup: Ann preferring the bad boy Riggins character
jokes.punchline: Leslie's moral judgment of fictional character
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character moment showing her black-and-white morality extending to TV

jokes.text: I almost bought a toe ring the other day. What? Ann, you're somebody's mother.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentioning something she wants to talk about
jokes.punchline: Her horrified reaction to Ann considering a toe ring as a mother
jokes.explanation: Leslie's moral panic over a toe ring shows her conservative side about motherhood. The escalation from casual mention to moral outrage works.

jokes.text: When it's all said and done, there were a few mazes I'd cheated on. Also, the inside of my mouth is black and hot.
jokes.setup: Andy reflecting on his dentist experience
jokes.punchline: His confession about cheating on magazine puzzles combined with dental discomfort
jokes.explanation: Andy's confession about cheating combined with his dental description is perfectly random and childlike. Good callback to earlier setup.

jokes.text: So do I strip down to my underwear, or all the way naked?
jokes.setup: Andy at the dentist
jokes.punchline: His confusion about dental procedure clothing requirements
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy confusion - applying wrong medical procedure expectations to dental work. Shows his fundamental misunderstanding of basic situations.

jokes.text: Gentlemen, I've won two online satellite Jamaican poker tournaments. I can tell that you're bluffing.
jokes.setup: Harvey claiming to know they're bluffing
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously specific and fake-sounding poker credentials
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harvey moment - the oddly specific and probably fabricated poker credentials delivered with complete confidence. 'Satellite Jamaican' is particularly absurd.

jokes.text: I am. And I was bluffing about winning those tournaments.
jokes.setup: Harvey's poker credentials being questioned
jokes.punchline: His immediate admission that he made everything up
jokes.explanation: Nice immediate deflation of Harvey's bluster. His honesty about lying is unexpectedly refreshing and shows he's not completely delusional.

jokes.text: Second of all, I got a Tin Cup Blu-Ray itching for a Rene Russo signature.
jokes.setup: Tom negotiating terms
jokes.punchline: His personal request for celebrity memorabilia from the tents
jokes.explanation: Good callback and Tom character moment, though somewhat expected

jokes.text: Oh, hey. I'm Larry. Great to know you. I love tent world.
jokes.setup: Larry being introduced as the guy who brought Harvey down
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic, friendly greeting to the man he just defeated
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - his innocent friendliness even toward someone who was scamming them. Shows his genuine, oblivious nature.

jokes.text: God! Can you just be cool for one second?
jokes.setup: Larry being friendly to Harvey
jokes.punchline: Tom's frustrated plea for Larry to read the room
jokes.explanation: Standard Tom frustration with Larry's social obliviousness. Gets the job done but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Press the paw. Hi, Leslie. It's Ann. I love you, and everything's gonna be okay.
jokes.setup: April and Donna giving Leslie a gift
jokes.punchline: The sweet but slightly patronizing recorded message
jokes.explanation: Sweet gesture that's also slightly condescending - treating Leslie like a child who needs comfort. Shows their friendship in a tangible way.

jokes.text: Mm, you touch me and I stab the bunny.
jokes.setup: Leslie suggesting a five-minute hug tradition
jokes.punchline: April's threat to destroy the gift to avoid physical affection
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - threatening violence against an innocent object to avoid human affection. The economy and character specificity are excellent.

jokes.text: I'm just an impartial bunny, but I think Ann sucks. Also-- I'm the zodiac killer.
jokes.setup: April presumably making the bunny 'talk'
jokes.punchline: The bunny's increasingly dark messages
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April moment - using the innocent gift to express her dark thoughts. The escalation from hating Ann to serial killer confession is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Also-- I'm the zodiac killer.
jokes.setup: April making the bunny voice her opinions
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurd criminal confession
jokes.explanation: Excellent April escalation - perfect dark humor and absurdist confession. Highly quotable and completely unexpected

jokes.text: Your inhaler is hidden under the pile of pistachio shells on my desk, obviously.
jokes.setup: Larry looking for his inhaler
jokes.punchline: Tom's sarcastic suggestion involving his pistachio habit
jokes.explanation: Sets up the allergy joke but is mostly functional. Tom's sarcasm is in character but not particularly inspired.

jokes.text: Okay, well, that's funny, 'cause I'm terribly allergic to pistachios.
jokes.setup: Tom mentioning pistachios sarcastically
jokes.punchline: Larry's serious allergy revelation making Tom look bad
jokes.explanation: Good reversal - Tom's casual cruelty backfires when Larry's genuine medical condition makes him look terrible. Sets up the defense speech nicely.

jokes.text: I like Larry. He is a good friend and a fine man with an inexplicably gorgeous wife-- I mean, to the point where it makes no sense at all.
jokes.setup: Ben defending Larry
jokes.punchline: His genuine defense derailed by confusion about Gayle's attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Ben's genuine desire to defend Larry gets sidetracked by his honest bewilderment. The self-correction makes it funnier.

jokes.text: O, captain, my captain.
jokes.setup: Ben's defense of Larry getting passionate
jokes.punchline: His dramatic Dead Poets Society reference for Larry
jokes.explanation: Ben's dramatic escalation using literary references for Larry. The mismatch between high culture and Larry is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: Got so startled I tooted.
jokes.setup: Larry being moved by Ben's defense
jokes.punchline: His embarrassing physical reaction to emotional moments
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - his emotional response manifests physically in an embarrassing way. The word 'tooted' is appropriately childish.
